2605.13674.title: Weakly Supervised Segmentation as Semantic-Based Regularization
2605.13674.paper_summary: Weakly supervised semantic segmentation (WSSS) trains dense pixel-level segmentation models from partial or coarse annotations such as bounding boxes, scribbles, or image-level tags. While recent work leverages foundation models such as the Segment Anything Model (SAM) to generate pseudo-labels, these approaches typically depend on heuristic prompt choices and offer limited ways to incorporate prior knowledge or heterogeneous labels. We address this gap by taking a neurosymbolic perspective: integrating differentiable fuzzy logic with deep segmentation models. Weak annotations and domain-specific priors are unified as continuous logical constraints that fine-tune SAM under weak supervision. The refined foundation model then produces improved pseudo-labels, from which we train a second-stage prompt-free segmentation model. Experiments on Pascal VOC 2012 and the REFUGE2 optic disc/cup segmentation dataset show that our logic-guided fine-tuning yields higher-quality pseudo-labels, leading to state-of-the-art segmentation accuracy that often exceeds densely supervised baselines.
2605.13674.paper_summary_zh: 弱監督語義分割（WSSS）從部分或粗略標註（例如邊界框、塗鴉或圖像級標籤）訓練密集的像素級分割模型。雖然最近的研究利用基礎模型，如Segment Anything Model（SAM），來生成偽標籤，但這些方法通常依賴於啟發式提示選擇，並提供有限的方式來整合先驗知識或異質標籤。我們通過採取神經符號的視角來解決這一問題：將可微分的模糊邏輯與深度分割模型相結合。弱標註和特定領域的先驗知識統一為連續的邏輯約束，這些約束在弱監督下微調SAM。然後，經過精煉的基礎模型生成改進的偽標籤，從中我們訓練第二階段無提示的分割模型。在Pascal VOC 2012和REFUGE2視神經盤/杯分割數據集上的實驗顯示，我們的邏輯引導微調產生了更高質量的偽標籤，從而導致最先進的分割準確性，通常超過密集監督的基準。
2605.13674.author: Stefano Colamonaco et.al.
2605.13674.authors: Stefano Colamonaco, Andrei-Bogdan Florea, Jaron Maene
